meaning: adv.向上游,溯流,逆流地adj.溯流而上的
examples: The falls upstream are full of salmon.
We'll go to the upstream side of that big rock.
Salmon manage to swim upstream to lay their eggs.
That means that the euro will have to swim upstream for another three to four years.
Mark Druskoff is based in Houston and covers midstream and upstream oil and gas.
